thesis: Large multi-agent systems coordinate to optimal effect when they become proof-governed sovereign institutions.

coordinationLaw.plainLanguage: Optimal effect means more verified work, lower risk, lower coordination waste, and reusable memory — not more agents for its own sake.
coordinationLaw.equation: maximize verified value minus compute cost, safety risk, coordination overhead, and unknown externality

stages.label: Mission
stages.description: The objective is bounded into a mission contract with scope, risk, and acceptance gates.

stages.label: Constellation
stages.description: The coordinator activates only the agents needed for the mission.

stages.label: Work
stages.description: Agents execute bounded roles under tool, cost, and authority constraints.

stages.label: Proof
stages.description: Every claim becomes a proof packet, trace root, or evidence object.

stages.label: Validate
stages.description: Verifier agents, risk sentinels, and human authority gates accept, reject, or escalate.

stages.label: Chronicle
stages.description: Accepted proof becomes institutional memory and routing experience.

stages.label: Capability
stages.description: Reusable capability is admitted only if evidence survives the gate.

agents.name: Coordinator
agents.role: selects constellation, budget, access graph, and stopping rule

agents.name: Planner
agents.role: turns the objective into subgoals and acceptance criteria

agents.name: Researcher
agents.role: maps public-safe evidence and source provenance

agents.name: Validator
agents.role: checks evidence, baselines, replay, and proof integrity

agents.name: Risk Sentinel
agents.role: blocks unsafe propagation and unresolved claim debt

agents.name: Chronicle
agents.role: preserves accepted experience and capability lineage

agents.name: Human Authority
agents.role: final acceptance gate and escalation boundary
